steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure needed
steps.content:
The sentence reads: “The Suzuki method is an approach to music instruction ______ to teach music in the same natural way children learn language, emphasizes playing instruments from a very young age.” The word “emphasizes” is already given as the main verb of the relative clause. That means the blank must supply a relative pronoun (“that”) plus a participial phrase — not another finite verb — so that “that” remains the subject and “emphasizes” remains the predicate.
  • Choice A — ‘that, is aiming’: inserts the finite verb “is aiming” before “emphasizes,” creating two competing main verbs in the relative clause. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘that has aimed’: inserts the finite verb “has aimed” before “emphasizes,” same problem of two competing predicates. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘that, aiming’: the participial phrase “aiming to teach music in the same natural way children learn language” is set off by commas as a parenthetical, leaving “that” as subject and “emphasizes” as sole main verb. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘that aims’: inserts “aims” without the comma needed before “emphasizes,” producing two finite verbs without proper punctuation to separate them. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only “that, aiming” makes the participial phrase a parenthetical, preserving “emphasizes” as the one main verb in the clause. That matches choice C — C is correct.
